desires: Get that term project submitted by Thursday without anyone noticing I barely started it.
Escape the anxiety loop before my brain actually crashes from scrolling at 3 AM.
Finish this trivial chore so I can feel 'productive' enough to justify ignoring the big deadline.

goals.description: Finish the trivial chore completely now to lock in that 'instant win' feeling, then immediately block out all thoughts about the term project.
goals.rationale: I need this dopamine hit. Finishing this tiny bit proves I'm being productive without actually touching the hard stuff yet. If I don't get this done tonight, someone will ask why I haven't started the real thing, and I'll have to explain it in front of my friends later. That's too much friction. Plus, once this is done, I can just scroll for an hour and forget everything until tomorrow when I can pretend I didn't do anything.

goals.rationale: This feels like a low priority unless things go wrong. My friends are generally chill, so as long as I don't drop a bombshell on them, I should be fine. It's better to focus on getting the easy task done first.
